name: Tombigbee
description_short: Tombigbee Electric Cooperative is a rural utility cooperative in Northwest Alabama providing electricity, prepaid metering, and fiber internet services to member communities.
description_long:
Tombigbee Electric Cooperative is a member-owned utility cooperative serving rural Northwest Alabama. Founded on cooperative principles, it operates as a not-for-profit organization governed by a board of directors and managed according to established bylaws and the Touchstone Energy cooperative framework. The organization is part of a broader cooperative movement emphasizing community benefit and member control.

The cooperative's primary service is electricity distribution to residential and commercial members in its service territory. Beyond basic utility service, Tombigbee offers prepaid metering through its TakeCHARGE program, multiple billing and payment options including bill pay assistance programs, and outdoor lighting services. The cooperative has recently launched Freedom Fiber, a subsidiary providing fiber-optic internet access to rural areas of Northwest Alabama. Members can access account management, report outages, and sign up for outage alerts via mobile apps and online portals.

Tombigbee distinguishes itself through its cooperative ownership structure, which means profits and benefits accrue to member-owners rather than external shareholders. The organization emphasizes community engagement through scholarships, youth educational programs, and local news/events. As a cooperative, it operates under principles of mutual aid and transparency, with governance directly involving member participation through the board structure.

However, Tombigbee is primarily an electric utility company, not a consumer finance provider. While it offers billing assistance programs and payment options, these are ancillary services supporting electricity delivery, not primary financial products. The company was miscategorized in the payday-alternatives category, which is designed for short-term lending products and credit solutions. Tombigbee's core business is utility service delivery, making banking the more accurate categorization only insofar as it handles member accounts and payments—not consumer lending.
services: Residential and commercial electricity distribution and service
TakeCHARGE prepaid metering program for usage control
Bill pay assistance programs for qualifying members
Online account access and management portal
Mobile app for account management and outage reporting
New service applications and engineering forms
pros: Member-owned cooperative structure means profits and governance benefits accrue to member-owners
Multiple payment options available including bill pay assistance programs for qualifying members
TakeCHARGE prepaid metering program offers control over electricity usage and costs
Fiber internet subsidiary (Freedom Fiber) expanding broadband access to underserved rural areas
Touchstone Energy affiliation provides additional cooperative benefits and standards
Community-focused programs including member scholarships and youth educational opportunities
Outage texting and mobile app services for real-time utility management
cons: Not a financial services provider—cannot offer payday alternatives, personal loans, or credit products
Service territory limited to Northwest Alabama, not available nationally or in other regions
Website experienced billing system disruptions (payments disabled February 24–March 2) indicating potential technical limitations
Limited online functionality—some account services require phone contact at (205) 921-6712
As a utility company, subject to regulatory constraints on pricing and service offerings
best_for: Rural Alabama residents needing reliable electricity and utility services in the Tombigbee service area
Cooperative members seeking community-based utility ownership and governance participation
Rural broadband users interested in fiber internet expansion in Northwest Alabama
diagnosis: Tombigbee is an electric utility cooperative serving rural Northwest Alabama, not a consumer finance company. It was incorrectly categorized as a payday-alternative lender. While it offers bill payment assistance as a utility member service, it does not provide loans, credit products, or financial alternatives. This profile should be removed from consumer finance databases or recategorized as a utility/banking service provider only for members in its service territory.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
